rationale: The cited paper is directly about mechanisms for how brand personality impressions are formed/updated (trait vs. evaluative inferencing, trait accessibility), and the citing paper explicitly states a gap in understanding how brand personality is created and tests an antecedent (advertising spokesperson) for MBP/FBP perceptions. Given this strong construct-level overlap and plausible theoretical use, this is not clearly an incidental mention. The DOI-level match confirms the specific reference (10.1509/jmkr.2005.42.4.458).
